title: Dense Moroccan Pilchards Tagine
description: A heartwarming, peppery, and nostalgic Moroccan dish featuring pilchards, perfect for a low-fat friendly meal. Traditionally cooked in a mortar and pestle for a rich and intense flavor.
instructions: Rinse the pilchards and drain them well.
Heat the olive oil in a large mortar and pestle over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute.
Add the cumin, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the pilchards and cook until they start to flake apart, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped parsley, lemon juice, water, and chopped cilantro. Stir well to combine.
Continue cooking for another 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the pilchards are cooked through.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the Dense Moroccan Pilchards Tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
Cover and refrigerate any leftovers for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months.
Reheat the pilchards in a saucepan over low heat, adding a little water if the sauce has thickened too much.
